memberdef.argsstring: (SYN_BLAKE2s *ctx, const void *key, size_t keylen, size_t outlen)
memberdef.name: syn_blake2s_init_keyed

briefdescription.para: Initialize BLAKE2s for keyed hashing (MAC mode).

detaileddescription.para: After calling this, the context must be re-initialized before reuse.

parameterdescription.para: Output buffer (must be at least ctx->outlen bytes).

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *data, size_t len, uint8_t *out, size_t outlen)
memberdef.name: syn_blake2s

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *key, size_t keylen, const void *data, size_t len, uint8_t *out, size_t outlen)
memberdef.name: syn_blake2s_mac

briefdescription.para: Feed message data into the HMAC.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *key, size_t keylen, const void *data, size_t datalen, uint8_t mac[SYN_BLAKE2S_MAX_DIGEST])
memberdef.name: syn_hmac_blake2s

briefdescription.para: BLAKE2s cryptographic hash — RFC 7693, pure C99.

detaileddescription.para: Supports both unkeyed hashing and keyed MAC mode. WireGuard uses both: unkeyed BLAKE2s-256 for chaining hash, keyed BLAKE2s for MAC.
Context is caller-owned (~120 bytes on 32-bit targets). No heap.
Also provides HMAC-BLAKE2s as static inline functions — used internally by the WireGuard module for HKDF key derivation.
